_format.description: Looping records for flight-path task tracking. Not strings, not logs — structured divergent records that compress on merge and keep differences as wings.

fields.generation: merge generation, 0 for first flight, +1 per merge
fields.parent_ids: list of step_ids this record merged from; empty for first flights
fields.what: one-line summary of the successful step
fields.why: reason, evidence, causal link to goal
fields.common: fields shared with parents (empty on first flight; populated on merge by intersecting parent fields)

wings.description: divergent branches; each wing is a named object holding fields that differ across merged parents

fields.next_flight: pointer to next expected step_id (may be null)
fields.fallback_from: pointer to a predecessor if this record is a retry/rewound flight

_format.merge_rule: merge(a, b) -> new record r where r.common = intersect(a.fields, b.fields), r.wings = { a: diff(a, r.common), b: diff(b, r.common) }, r.parent_ids = [a.step_id, b.step_id], r.generation = max(a.generation, b.generation) + 1. Identical fields compress into common; divergent fields keep flying as wings.
